privileged_info_summary: The task relies on synthetically generating a stripped-down starter file ("transit_hub_starter.sh3d") from an official Sweet Home 3D sample ("SweetHome3DExample7.sh3d", Contemporary villa) by programmatically removing interior furniture and rooms while preserving walls and exterior features. All metadata claims for occupation, industry, points (100 total, 70 passing threshold), and the starter file name have been independently verified against the task scripts and verifier logic. However, a critical defect exists in the evaluation script ("export_result.sh"): the script attempts to count newly added doors using the obsolete "<pieceOfFurniture doorOrWindow=\"true\">" XML pattern, whereas modern versions of Sweet Home 3D (including 7.5 used in this environment) natively serialize doors and windows as "<doorOrWindow>" tags. Because the parser explicitly ignores this tag, it will persistently report 0 new doors regardless of how many the agent places, causing the 20-point C2 grading condition ("new_walls >= 3 and new_doors >= 4") to permanently fail. Given the 70-point passing threshold out of 100 total points, it remains theoretically possible for an agent to pass the task by achieving a perfect score on all other grading conditions (which sum to a maximum of 80 achievable points).
